regime_narrative: Range-bound for 11 days now. BTC $66,300-$69,400 with declining volume. The longer this consolidation runs, the more significant the eventual resolution. No directional bias yet.

scorecard_summary.narrative: Solid week. 67% hit rate on triggered setups. High-confidence calls continue to outperform. Two of the three invalidations were wrong_level — triggers set 1-2% too tight. That's a pattern to watch.

week_narrative: This was a week about ETH. Monday's funding creep set up Tuesday's liquidation cascade. The rest of the week was about whether the damage was done. It was — $1,820 held cleanly, and by Friday ETH had recovered 60% of the move. BTC did nothing all week, which is its own kind of data point. SOL continues relative strength but hasn't broken out.
regime_assessment: No regime change. The consolidation is aging — 11 days is long for crypto. Volume declining, Bollinger bandwidth compressing. The setup for a volatility expansion is building but there's no directional signal yet. The range is well-defined and respected.
what_would_change_my_mind: Range-bound thesis holds until proven otherwise. A daily close above $70,000 with rising OI would make me bullish. A daily close below $65,000 on elevated volume would turn me bearish. Until one of those happens, this is a range and should be traded as one.

setups.narrative: Range breakout setup. Daily close above $70K with OI confirmation. This is the bull resolution of the 11-day range.
Range breakdown setup. Daily close below $65K on volume. The bear resolution.
Mean reversion if RSI gets oversold on the hourly. Post-cascade recovery has been reliable — 3 of last 4 RSI oversold signals on ETH led to 3%+ bounces within 48h.

assets.narrative: Range-bound, day 11. $66,300-$69,400 with declining volume. Bollinger bandwidth compressing to levels that typically precede 5%+ moves. No directional bias — watching for the breakout.

Recovering from Tuesday's liquidation cascade. $1,862 with funding normalizing. Showed relative weakness all week — underperformed BTC by 3%. Worth watching whether the recovery has legs or if this is just a dead cat bounce.

Three consecutive weeks of relative strength. Diverging from the alt pack. $141 with the $144 resistance still unbroken. The persistence of the relative strength makes the eventual breakout more interesting.
Nothing to say. Range-bound with BTC, no independent price action.

beliefs: BTC range-bound, day 11. Holding $66,300-$69,400 on declining volume.
ETH showing relative weakness vs BTC across all timeframes for 2 weeks.
SOL relative strength for 3 weeks, diverging from alt pack.
Bollinger bandwidth compression suggests volatility expansion imminent (1-2 weeks).
Funding rates normalizing across the board — no directional pressure from derivatives.
Range resolution will likely be violent given the compression duration.
biases: Calling ETH breakdowns too early on funding signals alone. Funding + declining OI has been unreliable (2/7 hit rate).
Breakout trigger levels consistently 1-2% too tight. 4 of last 6 breakout misses came within 1% of trigger before reversing.
Tendency to overweight SOL relative strength — it's been right but the breakout hasn't materialized.
hypotheses: Range resolves upward if BTC closes above $70K with rising OI (stated week 2, still active, no evidence yet).
ETH underperformance is institutional rotation, not fundamental weakness (stated week 2, evidence mixed — funding normalized but relative weakness persists).
Bollinger bandwidth at current levels has preceded 5%+ moves within 2 weeks in 4 of last 5 instances (stated this week, tracking).
